title: Broken even-number check
code:
def is_even(n: int) -> bool:
    return n % 2 == 1
reference_explanation: The function reverses the parity check. `n % 2 == 1` identifies odd numbers, so the function returns false for even values and true for odd values.
reference_fix:
def is_even(n: int) -> bool:
    return n % 2 == 0

title: Quadratic top-three ranking
reference_explanation: The function uses a nested-loop bubble sort just to get the top three scores. That makes the work O(n^2), which is wasteful for large datasets when Python already provides better options.
reference_fix:
def top_three(users: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    return sorted(users, key=lambda user: user["score"], reverse=True)[:3]

title: Unsafe user lookup
reference_explanation: The query is vulnerable to SQL injection because user input is interpolated directly into the SQL string. An attacker can break out of the quoted value and change the query logic.
explanation_keywords: user input changes the sql statement
attacker can manipulate the where clause

title: Plaintext password authentication
reference_explanation: The code compares raw plaintext passwords, which implies the application is storing or handling passwords in plaintext. If the credential store leaks, attackers can immediately reuse those secrets elsewhere.
explanation_keywords: passwords are not hashed or salted

title: React stale closure interval
reference_explanation: The interval callback closes over the initial `count` value, so each tick computes `0 + 1` again. The component needs either a functional state update or a dependency-aware effect.
bug_keywords: count is captured from the first render
explanation_keywords: the interval keeps using the old state value
the callback does not see fresh state

title: Repeated event listener registration
reference_explanation: The function registers the same event listener every second and never removes old listeners. That creates a memory leak and causes the message handler to fire multiple times for one event.

title: Path Traversal vulnerability
reference_explanation: The code blindly joins user input into a filesystem path. Attackers can use `../` segments to escape the uploads directory and read arbitrary files from the server.
explanation_keywords: user input can escape the upload directory
the joined path is not validated
fix_keywords: validate the path stays under the upload root

title: Django ORM N+1 queries
code:
from myapp.models import Author

def get_all_books():
    authors = Author.objects.all()
    return [author.book_set.all() for author in authors]
reference_explanation: This creates an N+1 query pattern: one query for authors, then another query for each author's books. The loop magnifies latency and load as the dataset grows.
reference_fix:
from myapp.models import Author

def get_all_books():
    authors = Author.objects.prefetch_related('book_set')
    return [author.book_set.all() for author in authors]
explanation_keywords: database round trips grow with the number of authors

title: Default submit behavior
code:
<form onsubmit="login()">
  <input type="text" name="user">
  <button class="action-btn" onclick="cancel()">Cancel</button>
  <button class="primary-btn">Login</button>
</form>
reference_explanation: Buttons inside a form default to `type="submit"`. That means the cancel button submits the form unexpectedly unless its type is set explicitly.
reference_fix:
<form onsubmit="login()">
  <input type="text" name="user">
  <button type="button" class="action-btn" onclick="cancel()">Cancel</button>
  <button type="submit" class="primary-btn">Login</button>
</form>
explanation_keywords: the browser treats button as submit inside a form
default html behavior causes the bug
fix_keywords: set the cancel button type explicitly

title: React layout infinite loop
reference_explanation: The layout effect depends on `width` and immediately sets `width` again, so each render triggers another render. Because `Math.random()` changes every time, the loop never stabilizes.
bug_keywords: effect updates a dependency it watches
explanation_keywords: every update triggers the effect again
Math.random creates a new width each render
fix_keywords: remove width from the dependency array
avoid setting width on every render

title: Go loop variable pointer leak
reference_explanation: The code appends the address of the loop variable `u`, not the address of each slice element. That makes every pointer reference the same memory slot, so the output repeats the last value.
bug_keywords: all pointers refer to the same value
explanation_keywords: the loop variable is reused across iterations
each pointer ends up pointing at the last user
fix_keywords: take the address of the slice element
